name: Paris
description:
Paris, the City of Light, is the capital and most populous city of France. Situated along the Seine River, Paris has been one of Europe's major centers of finance, diplomacy, commerce, culture, fashion, and gastronomy for centuries. Known for its romantic atmosphere and stunning architecture, Paris attracts over 30 million visitors annually, making it one of the most visited cities in the world.

The city's iconic landmarks include the Eiffel Tower, which was built in 1889 for the World's Fair and stands 330 meters tall, offering breathtaking panoramic views of the city. The Louvre Museum, housed in a former royal palace, is the world's largest art museum and home to thousands of works including the Mona Lisa and the Venus de Milo.

Paris is divided into 20 arrondissements (districts), each with its own unique character. The historic Marais district features medieval architecture and trendy boutiques, while Montmartre, perched on a hill in the north, is known for the white-domed Basilica of Sacre-Coeur and its artistic heritage — Picasso, Monet, and Van Gogh all lived and worked here.

French cuisine is considered among the finest in the world, and Paris is its epicenter. From flaky croissants and pain au chocolat at neighborhood bakeries to Michelin-starred restaurants, the city offers an unparalleled culinary experience. The cafe culture is central to Parisian life, with locals spending hours at sidewalk terraces sipping espresso and people-watching.

The city's extensive metro system makes getting around easy, with 16 lines covering every corner of the city. Paris also boasts numerous parks and gardens, including the Tuileries, Luxembourg Gardens, and the Bois de Boulogne, offering peaceful retreats from the bustling streets.
best_time: April to June, September to November
fun_fact: The Eiffel Tower grows about 6 inches taller in summer due to thermal expansion of the iron.
